title: I built an AI that turns your chart screenshot into a full trade setup (SL/TP included)
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
Hey everyone,

I’ve been building a tool recently and wanted to share it with the community to get some real feedback.

👉 [https://herculmarket.com/](https://herculmarket.com/)

The concept is simple:  
You upload a screenshot of your chart (TradingView, MT4/MT5…), and the AI gives you a structured analysis of your setup.

It includes:

* Entry zone
* Stop Loss / Take Profit
* Key support & resistance levels
* Invalidation point
* Confidence score

The goal isn’t to replace your analysis, but to give you a **fast, objective second opinion** before entering a trade.

It works across markets (Forex, crypto, indices, stocks), but I’m especially interested in feedback from active traders here.

A few questions:

* Would you actually use something like this in your workflow?
* What would make this kind of tool reliable for you?
* What’s missing in current trading tools you’ve tried?

If you test it, I’d genuinely appreciate your feedback — positive or negative.

Thanks 🙌

comments.author: stayhappyenjoylife
comments.body: Will it explain why it recommends the trade setup ?

replies.author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
replies.body:
Yes — that’s actually a core part of what I’m aiming for.

The idea isn’t just to suggest a setup, but to explain the reasoning behind it:

* key levels being respected or broken
* structure (trend, range, breakout…)
* and where the setup becomes invalid

Without that, it’s just a signal — and that’s not the goal.

comments.author: winglight2021
comments.body: I did this by a chrome plug-in to send the screenshot from tradingview tab to chatgpt tab. I realized that it won't work as AI can't predict any future just guess.

replies.author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
replies.body:
That’s fair — and I agree AI shouldn’t be seen as something that “predicts” the future.

The way I see it, the value isn’t in prediction, but in **consistency of analysis**.

Humans tend to interpret the same chart differently depending on context or bias. An AI can at least apply the same logic every time:

* identify structure
* highlight key levels
* define invalidation

So it’s less about guessing what will happen, and more about helping you assess whether a setup is actually valid or not.

In the end, the decision is still yours — but with a more objective lens.
